generatedSentence: The enigmatic wanderer, a man well past his prime, spent his days roaming from hostel to hostel, feeling perpetually unlucky and his efforts unfruitful. He carried nothing but the weight of nothingness, a sickly feeling in his gut, occasionally admiring the silky texture of a discarded lollipop wrapper. His keen sight could still discern the complex diagram of overlapping frameworks that seemed to exist twixt the worlds, like a ghostly halo around a tarnished knob. He wore threadbare underwear beneath his worn clothes, and his only comfort was a kilogram of vinted beer he’d sometimes receive, often showing his weary upper teeth in a half-hearted clown-like smile, reflecting on life’s strange throwbacks. A new threat emerged, a trenchant digital malware that spread like a violent shark through the net, its origins tracing back to rumored orichalc data streams. The old operative, his knee aching from years spent in a government bunker, found the situation unsatisfactory and felt an automatic warcry build within him. He remembered the erosion of past defenses, the need for a robust solution, not just another nursery rhyme. These new items, pulsing with ultraviolet energy, were of a different kind, deeply rooted. He saw the long, large reach of this cyberattack, like a shackle around the world's root systems, requiring a complete retrofitting of all digital defenses. He recalled a word whispered about a neutron bomb of data, its source steeped in digital mercury. He found himself aboard a clandestine server farm, where digital demons manifested as flickering faces on screens, a slick charcoal haze filling the air. This was no playable game; the very network had been pricked, forming a malevolent octet of servers. He performed a digital exorcism, metaphorically applying the discipline of karate, having kicked enough virtual buckets in his youth. The operation felt like a heavy weightlifting session against the arcane forces of witchcraft. A digital gecko virus, a dwarf in comparison to a full-blown attack, was the key. He had to judge the source, a shadowy gringo tech mogul whose intoxication with power echoed neolithic urges, nevertheless leading him to a corrupt foreign embassy where a former jeweler had been observed on the wrong track, not even koalas were safe from his greed. The digital threat was contained, bringing a quiet jubilee to the united networks, a truly munificent outcome. He found a new balance in his life, realizing he was a beneficiary of his own hard-won skills, and sought clarification on the remaining anomalies. An automatic internal laugh escaped him as he thought of the digital lions he’d faced, a retrospective of his long career, an instinct for justice his internal inking. He knew the world was still a patchwork of challenges, but he carried himself with a new gorget of confidence. The old grey warrior contemplated the funeral he'd almost attended, metaphorically speaking, but instead, he imagined flowers blooming. He made a mental note to valuate the new security protocols clockwise, finding a strangely crunchy satisfaction in his work, and considered buying some banjoes to finally unwind, for a word of warning had been given, and he had acted as the final judge.
panelSentences: The enigmatic wanderer, a man well past his prime, spent his days roaming from hostel to hostel, feeling perpetually unlucky and his efforts unfruitful. He carried nothing but the weight of nothingness, a sickly feeling in his gut, occasionally admiring the silky texture of a discarded lollipop wrapper. His keen sight could still discern the complex diagram of overlapping frameworks that seemed to exist twixt the worlds, like a ghostly halo around a tarnished knob. He wore threadbare underwear beneath his worn clothes, and his only comfort was a kilogram of vinted beer he’d sometimes receive, often showing his weary upper teeth in a half-hearted clown-like smile, reflecting on life’s strange throwbacks.

A new threat emerged, a trenchant digital malware that spread like a violent shark through the net, its origins tracing back to rumored orichalc data streams. The old operative, his knee aching from years spent in a government bunker, found the situation unsatisfactory and felt an automatic warcry build within him. He remembered the erosion of past defenses, the need for a robust solution, not just another nursery rhyme. These new items, pulsing with ultraviolet energy, were of a different kind, deeply rooted. He saw the long, large reach of this cyberattack, like a shackle around the world's root systems, requiring a complete retrofitting of all digital defenses. He recalled a word whispered about a neutron bomb of data, its source steeped in digital mercury.

He found himself aboard a clandestine server farm, where digital demons manifested as flickering faces on screens, a slick charcoal haze filling the air. This was no playable game; the very network had been pricked, forming a malevolent octet of servers. He performed a digital exorcism, metaphorically applying the discipline of karate, having kicked enough virtual buckets in his youth. The operation felt like a heavy weightlifting session against the arcane forces of witchcraft. A digital gecko virus, a dwarf in comparison to a full-blown attack, was the key. He had to judge the source, a shadowy gringo tech mogul whose intoxication with power echoed neolithic urges, nevertheless leading him to a corrupt foreign embassy where a former jeweler had been observed on the wrong track, not even koalas were safe from his greed.

The digital threat was contained, bringing a quiet jubilee to the united networks, a truly munificent outcome. He found a new balance in his life, realizing he was a beneficiary of his own hard-won skills, and sought clarification on the remaining anomalies. An automatic internal laugh escaped him as he thought of the digital lions he’d faced, a retrospective of his long career, an instinct for justice his internal inking. He knew the world was still a patchwork of challenges, but he carried himself with a new gorget of confidence. The old grey warrior contemplated the funeral he'd almost attended, metaphorically speaking, but instead, he imagined flowers blooming. He made a mental note to valuate the new security protocols clockwise, finding a strangely crunchy satisfaction in his work, and considered buying some banjoes to finally unwind, for a word of warning had been given, and he had acted as the final judge.
rawPanelSentences: The **enigmatic** wanderer, a man well past his prime, spent his days **roaming** from **hostel** to **hostel**, feeling perpetually **unlucky** and his efforts **unfruitful**. He carried **nothing** but the weight of **nothingness**, a **sickly** feeling in his gut, occasionally admiring the **silky** texture of a discarded **lollipop** wrapper. His keen **sight** could still discern the complex **diagram** of overlapping **frameworks** that seemed to exist **twixt** the worlds, like a ghostly **halo** around a tarnished **knob**. He wore threadbare **underwear** beneath his worn clothes, and his only comfort was a **kilogram** of **vinted** **beer** he’d sometimes **receive**, often **showing** his weary **upper** teeth in a half-hearted **clown**-like smile, reflecting on life’s strange **throwbacks**.

A new threat emerged, a **trenchant** digital **malware** that spread like a **violent** **shark** through the net, its origins tracing back to rumored **orichalc** data streams. The old operative, his **knee** aching from years spent in a government **bunker**, found the situation **unsatisfactory** and felt an **automatic** **warcry** build within him. He remembered the **erosion** of past defenses, the need for a **robust** solution, not just another **nursery** rhyme. These new **items**, pulsing with **ultraviolet** energy, were of a different **kind**, deeply rooted. He saw the **long**, **large** reach of this cyberattack, like a **shackle** around the world's **root** systems, requiring a complete **retrofitting** of all digital defenses. He recalled a **word** whispered about a **neutron** bomb of data, its source steeped in digital **mercury**.

He found himself **aboard** a clandestine server farm, where digital **demons** manifested as flickering **faces** on screens, a **slick** **charcoal** haze filling the air. This was no **playable** game; the very network had been **pricked**, forming a malevolent **octet** of servers. He performed a digital **exorcism**, metaphorically applying the discipline of **karate**, having **kicked** enough virtual **buckets** in his youth. The operation felt like a heavy **weightlifting** session against the arcane forces of **witchcraft**. A digital **gecko** virus, a **dwarf** in comparison to a full-blown attack, was the key. He had to **judge** the source, a shadowy **gringo** tech mogul whose **intoxication** with power echoed **neolithic** urges, **nevertheless** leading him to a corrupt foreign **embassy** where a former **jeweler** had been observed on the wrong **track**, not even **koalas** were safe from his greed.

The digital threat was contained, bringing a quiet **jubilee** to the **united** networks, a truly **munificent** outcome. He found a new **balance** in his life, realizing he was a **beneficiary** of his own hard-won skills, and sought **clarification** on the remaining anomalies. An **automatic** internal **laugh** escaped him as he thought of the digital **lions** he’d faced, a **retrospective** of his long career, an **instinct** for justice his internal **inking**. He knew the world was still a **patchwork** of challenges, but he carried himself with a new **gorget** of confidence. The old **grey** warrior contemplated the **funeral** he'd almost attended, metaphorically speaking, but instead, he imagined **flowers** blooming. He made a mental note to **valuate** the new security protocols **clockwise**, finding a strangely **crunchy** satisfaction in his work, and considered buying some **banjoes** to finally unwind, for a **word** of warning had been given, and he had acted as the final **judge**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A grizzled, middle-aged man with a prominent scar above his left eye and salt-and-pepper stubble, wearing a worn trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled man, sitting on a dilapidated cot in a dim hostel room, looking intently at a glowing, intricate digital diagram projected from a small device in his hand. Medium shot, slight low angle, emphasizing his weariness and the glow from the device. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The grizzled man, his face tense, standing before multiple monitors displaying chaotic, violent abstract malware patterns resembling shark fins and digital demons' faces, some with ultraviolet hues. Wide shot, showing him dwarfed by the glowing screens, reflections on his face. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled man, mid-action, striking a precise blow (like karate) at a server rack in a dark, high-tech server farm, sparks flying, a faint halo around his hand. Dynamic close-up, diagonal composition, showing his determined expression and the impact. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The grizzled man, standing against a backdrop of city lights at dawn, holding a single, blooming flower, a subtle, knowing smile on his mature face. Full shot, slightly from behind, looking out over the city, emphasizing a sense of peace and new beginnings. All panels feature cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting. All human characters are visibly adult with mature features.

definitions.definition: A mostly herbivorous insect of the order Orthoptera, noted for its ability to jump long distances and for the habit of some species communicating by stridulation; they are related to but distinct from crickets.
A cocktail made with crème de menthe and optionally with crème de cacao.
A young student in initial stages of training who has been chosen on account of their obvious talent.
In ordinary square or upright pianos of London make, the escapement lever or jack, so made that it can be taken out and replaced with the key.
Fitted to trench or cut; gutting; sharp.
Keen; biting; vigorously articulate and effective; severe.
definitions.example: trenchant wit

definitions.definition: A plan, drawing, sketch or outline to show how something works, or show the relationships between the parts of a whole.
definitions.example: Electrical diagrams show device interconnections.

definitions.definition: A graph or chart.

A functor from an index category to another category. The objects and morphisms of the index category need not have any internal substance, but rather merely outline the connective structure of at least some part of the diagram's codomain. If the index category is J and the codomain is C, then the diagram is said to be "of type J in C".
To represent or indicate something using a diagram.
To schedule the operations of a locomotive or train according to a diagram.
A support structure comprising joined parts or conglomerated particles and intervening open spaces of similar or larger size.
The arrangement of support beams that represent a building's general shape and size.
The larger branches of a tree that determine its shape.
A basic conceptual structure.
definitions.example: These ‘three principles of connexion’ compose the framework of principles in Hume's account of the association of ideas.

definitions.definition: A reusable piece of code (and, sometimes, other utilities) providing a standard environment within which an application can be implemented.
The identification and categorisation of processes or steps that constitute a complex task or mindset in order to render explicit the tacit and implicit.
A circular band of coloured light, visible around the sun or moon etc., caused by reflection and refraction of light by ice crystals in the atmosphere.
A cloud of gas and other matter surrounding and captured by the gravitational field of a large diffuse astronomical object, such as a galaxy or cluster of galaxies.
Anything resembling this band, such as an effect caused by imperfect developing of photographs.
Nimbus, a luminous disc, often of gold, around or over the heads of saints, etc., in religious paintings.
The metaphorical aura of glory, veneration or sentiment which surrounds an idealized entity.
(iconography) a circular annulus ring, frequently luminous, often golden, floating above the head
A circular brace used to keep the head and neck in position.
A rollbar placed in front of the driver, used to protect the cockpit of a open cockpit racecar.
To encircle with a halo.
A rounded protuberance, especially one arising from a flat surface; a fleshy lump or caruncle.
A rounded control switch that can be turned on its axis, designed to be operated by the fingers.
A ball-shaped part of a handle, lever, etc., designed to be grabbed by the hand.
A rounded ornament on the hilt of an edged weapon; a pommel.
A prominent, rounded bump along a mountain ridge.
A prominent rounded hill.
(chiefly in the plural) A woman's breast.
The penis.
The head of the penis; the glans.
(by analogy with above) A contemptible person.
A dollop, an amount just larger than a spoonful (usually referring to butter).
A chunky branch-like piece, especially of a ginger rhizome.
A bulb of the garlic plant consisting of multiple cloves.
(of a man) To have sex with.
An assailant.
To urge with violence.
Involving extreme force or motion.
definitions.example: A violent wind ripped the branch from the tree.

definitions.definition: Involving physical conflict.
definitions.example: We would rather negotiate, but we will use violent means if necessary.

definitions.definition: Likely to use physical force.
definitions.example: The escaped prisoners are considered extremely violent.

definitions.definition: Intensely vivid.
definitions.example: The artist expressed his emotional theme through violent colors.

definitions.definition: Produced or effected by force; not spontaneous; unnatural.
A scaleless, predatory fish of the superorder Selachimorpha, with a cartilaginous skeleton and 5 to 7 gill slits on each side of its head.
To fish for sharks.
Unfortunate, marked by misfortune.
Inauspicious.
Having ill luck.
Bringing ill luck.
Not bearing fruit.
definitions.example: Despite going on for two hours, her search was unfruitful.

definitions.definition: Clothes worn next to the skin, underneath outer clothing.
Underpants (boxers, briefs, panties, etc) and often bras.
(of a person or group) Very liberal in giving or bestowing.
(of a gift, donation, etc.) Very generous; lavish.
Software which has been designed to operate in a malicious, undesirable manner.
A valuable yellow metal known to the Ancient Greeks and Romans; now sometimes interpreted as referring to a natural alloy of gold and copper, and sometimes treated as a mythical substance.
The ritual act of driving out evil spirits from persons, places or things who are possessed by them.
Pertaining to an enigma.
Mysterious.
Defying description.
(variant) Enigmatical.
An item of confectionery consisting of a piece of candy/sweet attached to a stick.
To wander or travel freely and with no specific destination.
To use a network or service from different locations or devices.
To transmit (resources) between different locations or devices, to allow comparable usage from any of them.
To range or wander over.
definitions.example: Gangs of thugs roamed the streets.

definitions.definition: An instance of wandering.
The ability to use a cell phone outside of its original registering zone.
The use of a network or service from different locations or devices.
(operating system) Using the OS service of manipulating folders and documents from different PC devices by a registered user.
Something trifling, or of no consequence or importance.
A trivial remark (especially in the term sweet nothings).
A nobody (insignificant person).
definitions.example: You're nothing to me now!

definitions.definition: Not at all; in no way.
Not any thing; no thing.
An absence of anything, including empty space, brightness, darkness, matter, or a vacuum.
To ask for the presence or participation of someone or something.
definitions.example: We invited our friends round for dinner.

definitions.definition: To request formally.
definitions.example: I invite you all to be seated.

definitions.definition: To encourage.
definitions.example: I always invite criticism of my definitions.

definitions.definition: To allure; to draw to; to tempt to come; to induce by pleasure or hope; to attract.
Having been asked to attend.
Having an invitation.
Betwixt, between
(Jewish history) A special year of emancipation supposed to be kept every fifty years, when farming was abandoned and Hebrew slaves were set free.
A 25th, 40th, 50th, 60th or 70th anniversary.
A special year (originally held every hundred years, then fifty, and then fewer) in which remission from sin could be granted as well as indulgences upon making a pilgrimage to Rome.
A time of celebration or rejoicing.
An occasion of mass manumission from slavery.
A period of fifty years; a half-century.
A commercial overnight lodging place, with dormitory accommodation and shared facilities, especially a youth hostel
(not US) A temporary refuge for the homeless providing a bed and sometimes food
A small, unendowed college in Oxford or Cambridge.
To stay in a hostel as part of a travel

In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
The unit of weight such that a one-kilogram mass is also a one-kilogram weight.
An alcoholic drink fermented from starch material, commonly barley malt, often with hops or some other substance to impart a bitter flavor.
definitions.example: Beer is brewed all over the world.

definitions.definition: A fermented extract of the roots and other parts of various plants, as spruce, ginger, sassafras, etc.
A solution produced by steeping plant materials in water or another fluid.
A glass, bottle, or can of any of the above beverages.
definitions.example: Can I buy you a beer?

definitions.definition: A variety of the above beverages.
definitions.example: I haven't tried this beer before.

definitions.definition: To give beer to (someone)
An arched masonry structure supporting and forming a ceiling, whether freestanding or forming part of a larger building.
Any arched ceiling or roof.
Anything resembling such a downward-facing concave structure, particularly the sky and caves.
The space covered by an arched roof, particularly underground rooms and church crypts.
Any cellar or underground storeroom.
Any burial chamber, particularly those underground.
definitions.example: Family members had been buried in the vault for centuries.

definitions.definition: The secure room or rooms in or below a bank used to store currency and other valuables; similar rooms in other settings.
definitions.example: The bank kept their money safe in a large vault.

definitions.definition: A piece of apparatus used for performing jumps.
A gymnastic movement performed on this apparatus.
An encrypted digital archive.
An underground or covered conduit for water or waste; a drain; a sewer.
An underground or covered reservoir for water or waste; a cistern; a cesspit.
A room employing a cesspit or sewer: an outhouse; a lavatory.
To build as, or cover with a vault.
To make (something) sickly.
To become sickly.
Frequently ill or in poor health.
definitions.example: a sickly child

definitions.definition: Not in good health; (somewhat) sick.
(of a plant) Characterized by poor or unhealthy growth.
Appearing ill, infirm or unhealthy; giving the appearance of illness.
definitions.example: a sickly pallor

definitions.definition: Shedding a relatively small amount of light; (of light) not very bright.
Lacking intensity or vigour.
definitions.example: a sickly smile

definitions.definition: Associated with poor moral or mental well-being.
Tending to produce nausea.
definitions.example: a sickly smell; sickly sentimentality

definitions.definition: Overly sweet.
Marked by the occurrence of illness or disease (of a period of time).
Tending to produce disease or poor health.
definitions.example: a sickly autumn; a sickly climate

definitions.definition: In a sick manner; in a way that reflects or causes sickness.
definitions.example: sickly pale; to cough sickly

definitions.definition: A seal which can magically transform into a human by shedding its skin.
A chicken of a certain breed with very fine, silk-like feathers.
Similar in appearance or texture (especially in softness and smoothness) to silk.
definitions.example: cloth with a silky lustre

definitions.definition: Smooth and pleasant; seductive.
definitions.example: a silky voice

definitions.definition: Covered in long, slender, glistening hairs pressed close to the surface; sericeous.
(in the singular) The ability to see.
definitions.example: He is losing his sight and now can barely read.

definitions.definition: The act of seeing; perception of objects by the eye; view.
definitions.example: to gain sight of land

definitions.definition: Something seen.
Something worth seeing; a spectacle, either good or bad.
definitions.example: We went to London and saw all the sights – Buckingham Palace, Tower Bridge, and so on.

definitions.definition: A device used in aiming a projectile, through which the person aiming looks at the intended target.
A small aperture through which objects are to be seen, and by which their direction is settled or ascertained.
definitions.example: the sight of a quadrant

definitions.definition: A great deal, a lot; frequently used to intensify a comparative.
definitions.example: This is a darn sight better than what I'm used to at home!

definitions.definition: In a drawing, picture, etc., that part of the surface, as of paper or canvas, which is within the frame or the border or margin. In a frame, the open space, the opening.
The instrument of seeing; the eye.
Mental view; opinion; judgment.
definitions.example: In their sight it was harmless.

definitions.definition: To register visually.
To get sight of (something).
definitions.example: to sight land from a ship

definitions.definition: To apply sights to; to adjust the sights of; also, to give the proper elevation and direction to by means of a sight.
definitions.example: to sight a rifle or a cannon

definitions.definition: To take aim at.
To bring together as one.
definitions.example: I hope this song can unite people from all different cultures.

definitions.definition: To come together as one.
definitions.example: If we want to win, we will need to unite.

definitions.definition: Joined into a single entity.
Involving the joint activity of multiple agents.
Ultraviolet colour.
Of electromagnetic radiation beyond (higher in frequency than) light visible to the human eye; radiation with wavelengths from 380 nanometre - 10 nanometre
Inadequate, substandard or not satisfactory
A car with automatic transmission.
definitions.example: I never learned to drive a stick. I can only drive an automatic.

definitions.definition: A semi-automatic pistol.
definitions.example: The G-men raiding the speakeasy were equipped with .45 automatics, while the local policemen were carrying revolvers and shotguns.

definitions.definition: Capable of operating without external control or intervention.
definitions.example: The automatic clothes washer was a great labor-saving device.

definitions.definition: Done out of habit or without conscious thought.
definitions.example: Absent-minded doodling is a form of automatic art.

definitions.definition: Necessary, inevitable, prescribed by logic, law, etc.
definitions.example: Spitting at another player means an automatic red card.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm such as a machine gun) Firing continuously as long as the trigger is pressed until ammunition is exhausted.
definitions.example: Fully automatic weapons cannot be legally owned by private citizens in the US, except in very special circumstances, as by private security companies.

definitions.definition: (of a handgun) An autoloader; a semi-automatic or self-loading pistol, as opposed to a revolver or other manually actuated handgun, which fires one shot per pull of the trigger; distinct from machine guns.
definitions.example: The US Army adopted John Browning's M1911 pistol as its sidearm, chambered in .45 ACP (Automatic Colt Pistol).

definitions.definition: (of a local variable) Automatically added to and removed from the stack during the course of function calls.
(of a group) Having one or more finite-state automata
An exclamation intended to rally soldiers in battle, a battle cry.
The result of having been worn away or eroded, as by a glacier on rock or the sea on a cliff face.
The changing of a surface by mechanical action, friction, thermal expansion contraction, or impact.
The gradual loss of something as a result of an ongoing process.
definitions.example: the erosion of a person's trust

definitions.definition: Destruction by abrasive action of fluids.
(image processing) One of two fundamental operations in morphological image processing from which all other morphological operations are derived.
Loss of tooth enamel due to non-bacteriogenic chemical processes.
A shallow ulceration or lesion, usually involving skin or epithelial tissue.
In morphology, a basic operation (denoted ⊖); see Erosion (morphology).
Evincing strength and health; strong.
definitions.example: A robust wall was put up.

definitions.definition: Violent; rough; rude.
Requiring strength or vigor
definitions.example: robust employment

definitions.definition: Sensible (of intellect etc.); straightforward, not given to or confused by uncertainty or subtlety
Designed or evolved in such a way as to be resistant to total failure despite partial damage.
Resistant or impervious to failure regardless of user input or unexpected conditions.
Not greatly influenced by errors in assumptions about the distribution of sample errors.
A place where nursing or the raising of children is carried on.
A place where anything is fostered and growth promoted.
Something which educates and nurtures.
definitions.example: Commerce is the nursery of seamen.

definitions.definition: Short for nursery cannon.
Someone or something that is nursed; a nursling.
The act of nursing or rearing.
A subatomic particle forming part of the nucleus of an atom and having no charge; it is a combination of an up quark and two down quarks.
A distinct physical object.
definitions.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object that can be picked up for later use.
A line of text having a legal or other meaning; a separate particular in an account.
definitions.example: In response to the first item, we deny all wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (psychometrics) A question on a test, which may include its answers.
definitions.example: The exam has 100 items, each of which includes a correct response and three distractors.

definitions.definition: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
definitions.example: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

definitions.definition: Two people who are having a relationship with each other.
definitions.example: Jack and Jill are an item.

definitions.definition: A short article in a newspaper.
definitions.example: an item concerning the weather

definitions.definition: A hint; an innuendo.
To make a note of.
A reversion to an earlier stage of development.
A person considered to be primitive, uncivilized and mentally deficient.
An organism that has characteristics of a more primitive form.
An atavism.
A person similar to an ancestor, or something new similar to what already existed.
A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.
definitions.example: This is a strange kind of tobacco.

definitions.definition: A makeshift or otherwise atypical specimen.
definitions.example: The opening served as a kind of window.

definitions.definition: One's inherent nature; character, natural disposition.
Family, lineage.
Manner.
Goods or services used as payment, as e.g. in barter.
Equivalent means used as response to an action.
definitions.example: I'll pay in kind for his insult.

definitions.definition: Each of the two elements of the communion service, bread and wine.
In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.
definitions.example: Penny was wearing a miniskirt, so she skinned her exposed knees when she fell.

definitions.definition: In the horse and allied animals, the carpal joint, corresponding to the wrist in humans.
The part of a garment that covers the knee.
A piece of timber or metal formed with an angle somewhat in the shape of the human knee when bent.
An act of kneeling, especially to show respect or courtesy.
definitions.example: To make a knee.

definitions.definition: Any knee-shaped item or sharp angle in a line; an inflection point.
definitions.example: the knee of a graph

definitions.definition: A blow made with the knee; a kneeing.
To kneel to.
definitions.example: 1605: I could as well be brought / To knee his throne and, squire-like, pension beg / To keep base life afoot. — William Shakespeare, King Lear II.ii

definitions.definition: To poke or strike with the knee.
To move on the knees; to use the knees to move.
A vessel for boiling a liquid or cooking food, usually metal and equipped with a lid.
definitions.example: There's a hot kettle of soup on the stove.

definitions.definition: The quantity held by a kettle.
A vessel for boiling water for tea.
definitions.example: Stick the kettle on and we'll have a nice cup of tea.

definitions.definition: A kettle hole, sometimes any pothole.
(collective) A group of raptors riding a thermal, especially when migrating.
definitions.example: a kettle of hawks

definitions.definition: A steam locomotive
A kettledrum.
An instance of kettling; a group of protesters or rioters confined in a limited area.
(of the police) To contain demonstrators in a confined area.
A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
Mental equilibrium; mental health; calmness, a state of remaining clear-headed and unperturbed.
Something of equal weight used to provide equilibrium; counterweight.
definitions.example: Blair thought he could provide a useful balance to Bush's policies.

definitions.definition: A pair of scales.
Awareness of both viewpoints or matters; neutrality; rationality; objectivity.
The overall result of conflicting forces, opinions etc.; the influence which ultimately "weighs" more than others.
definitions.example: I think the balance of opinion is that we should get out while we're ahead.

definitions.definition: Apparent harmony in art (between differing colours, sounds, etc.).
A list accounting for the debits on one side, and for the credits on the other.
The result of such a procedure; the difference between credit and debit of an account.
definitions.example: I just need to nip to a bank and check my balance.

definitions.definition: A device used to regulate the speed of a watch, clock etc.
The remainder.
definitions.example: The balance of the agreement remains in effect.

definitions.definition: Libra.
To bring (items) to an equipoise, as the scales of a balance by adjusting the weights.
To make (concepts) agree.
To hold (an object or objects) precariously; to support on a narrow base, so as to keep from falling.
definitions.example: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.

definitions.definition: To compare in relative force, importance, value, etc.; to estimate.
(dancing) To move toward, and then back from, reciprocally.
definitions.example: to balance partners

definitions.definition: To contract, as a sail, into a narrower compass.
definitions.example: to balance the boom mainsail

definitions.definition: To make the credits and debits of (an account) correspond.
definitions.example: This final payment, or credit, balances the account.

definitions.definition: To be in equilibrium.
To have matching credits and debits.
To weigh in a balance.
To hesitate or fluctuate.
One who benefits or receives an advantage.
definitions.example: You are the lucky beneficiary of this special offer.

definitions.definition: One who benefits from the distribution, especially of an estate.
definitions.example: If any beneficiary does not survive the Settlor for a period of 30 days then the Trustee shall distribute that beneficiary’s share to the surviving beneficiaries by right of representation.

definitions.definition: One who benefits from the payout of an insurance policy.
Holding some office or valuable possession, in subordination to another; holding under a feudal or other superior; having a dependent and secondary possession.
Bestowed as a gratuity.
definitions.example: beneficiary gifts

definitions.definition: A stringed musical instrument (chordophone), usually with a round body, a membrane-like soundboard and a fretted neck, played by plucking or strumming the strings.
definitions.example: I come from Alabama with my banjo on my knee...

definitions.definition: Any of various similar musical instruments, such as the Tuvan doshpuluur, with a membrane-like soundboard.
An object shaped like a banjo, especially a frying pan or a shovel.
(Dagenham) A cul-de-sac with a round end.
A hardened shelter, often buried partly or fully underground, designed to protect the inhabitants from falling bombs or other attacks.
A large container or bin for storing coal, often built outside in the yard of a house. Now rare, as different types of fuels and energy sources are being used.
A container for storing coal or fuel oil for a ship's engine. [Also, by extension] the quantity of fuel needed to replenish that container.
The coal compartment on a tank engine.
A sand-filled hollow on a golf course.
An obstacle used to block an opposing player's view and field of fire.
A sort of chest or box, as in a window, the lid of which serves for a seat.
A kitchen worktop.
One who bunks off; a truant from school.
Certain fish, menhaden.
To load a vessel with oil or coal for the engine.
To hit a golf ball into a bunker.

To fire constantly at a hiding opponent, preventing them from firing at other players and trapping them behind the barrier. This can also refer to eliminating an opponent behind cover by rushing the position and firing at extremely close range as the player becomes exposed.
To make wine from fruit
To display, to have somebody see (something).
definitions.example: All he had to show for four years of attendance at college was a framed piece of paper.

definitions.definition: To bestow; to confer.
To indicate (a fact) to be true; to demonstrate.
To guide or escort.
definitions.example: Could you please show him on his way. He has overstayed his welcome.

definitions.definition: To be visible; to be seen; to appear.
definitions.example: At length, his gloom showed.

definitions.definition: To put in an appearance; show up.
definitions.example: We waited for an hour, but they never showed.

definitions.definition: To have an enlarged belly and thus be recognizable as pregnant.
(racing) To finish third, especially of horses or dogs.
definitions.example: In the third race: Aces Up won, paying eight dollars; Blarney Stone placed, paying three dollars; and Cinnamon showed, paying five dollars.

definitions.definition: To have a certain appearance, such as well or ill, fit or unfit; to become or suit; to appear.
An occasion when something is shown.
definitions.example: We went to the midnight showing of the new horror movie.

definitions.definition: A result, a judgement.
definitions.example: He made a poor showing at his first time at bat.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A restraint fit over a human or animal appendage, such as a wrist, ankle or finger; normally used in pairs joined by a chain.
definitions.example: The prisoner lay in shackles in his gloomy cell.

definitions.definition: A U-shaped piece of metal secured with a pin or bolt across the opening, or a hinged metal loop secured with a quick-release locking pin mechanism.
(usually in the plural) A restraint on one's action, activity, or progress.
A fetter-like band worn as an ornament.
A link for connecting railroad cars; a drawlink or draglink.
A length of cable or chain equal to 12 1/2 fathoms or 75 feet, or later to 15 fathoms.
Stubble.
A stimulant, such as amphetamine, that increases energy and decreases appetite.
The upper portion of something
Someone with higher social standing
That which is higher, contrasted with the lower.
A spiritual passageway through which consciousness can reach a higher dimension.
At a higher level, rank or position.
Situated on higher ground, further inland, or more northerly.
(of strata or geological time periods) younger, more recent
Of or pertaining to a secondary school.
A slapstick performance artist often associated with a circus and usually characterized by bright, oversized clothing, a red nose, face paint, and a brightly colored wig.
A person who acts in a silly fashion.
A stupid person.
A man of coarse nature and manners; an awkward fellow; an illbred person; a boor.
One who works upon the soil; a rustic; a churl; a yokel.
To act in a silly or playful fashion.
To ridicule.

The act of clarifying; the act or process of making clear or transparent by freeing visible impurities; particularly, the clearing or fining of liquid substances from feculent matter by the separation of the insoluble particles which prevent the liquid from being transparent.
definitions.example: The clarification of wine.

definitions.definition: The act of freeing from obscurities.
definitions.example: Your ideas deserve clarification.

definitions.definition: A form of exercise in which weights are lifted.
A sport in which competitors lift heavy weights in two events: the snatch and the clean and jerk.
The practice of witches; magic, sorcery or the use of supernatural powers to influence or predict events.
definitions.example: Wiccans believe in a modernised form of witchcraft.

definitions.definition: Something, such as an advanced technology, that seems almost magical.
A long vowel.
A long syllable.
A note formerly used in music, one half the length of a large, twice that of a breve.
A long integer variable, twice the size of an int, two or four times the size of a short, and half of a long long.
An entity with a long position in an asset.
definitions.example: Every uptick made the longs cheer.

definitions.definition: A long-term investment.
The long summer vacation at the English universities.
To take a long position in.
Having much distance from one terminating point on an object or an area to another terminating point (usually applies to horizontal dimensions; see Usage Notes below).
definitions.example: It's a long way from the Earth to the Moon.

definitions.definition: Having great duration.
definitions.example: The pyramids of Egypt have been around for a long time.

definitions.definition: Seemingly lasting a lot of time, because it is boring or tedious or tiring.
Not short; tall.
Possessing or owning stocks, bonds, commodities or other financial instruments with the aim of benefiting of the expected rise in their value.
definitions.example: I'm long in DuPont;  I have a long position in DuPont.

definitions.definition: Of a fielding position, close to the boundary (or closer to the boundary than the equivalent short position).
(of a ball or a shot) Landing beyond the baseline, and therefore deemed to be out.
definitions.example: That forehand is long.

definitions.definition: Occurring or coming after an extended interval; distant in time; far away.
An old musical note, equal to two longas, four breves, or eight semibreves.
Liberality, generosity.
(plural: large) A thousand dollars/pounds.
definitions.example: Getting a car tricked out like that will cost you 50 large.

definitions.definition: A large serving of something.
definitions.example: One small coffee and two larges, please.

definitions.definition: Of considerable or relatively great size or extent.
definitions.example: Russia is a large country.   The fruit-fly has large eyes for its body size.   He has a large collection of stamps.

definitions.definition: Abundant; ample.
Full in statement; diffuse; profuse.
Free; unencumbered.
Unrestrained by decorum; said of language.
Crossing the line of a ship's course in a favorable direction; said of the wind when it is abeam, or between the beam and the quarter.
Before the wind.
An operation in which data is received.
To take, as something that is offered, given, committed, sent, paid, etc.; to accept; to be given something.
definitions.example: She received many presents for her birthday.

definitions.definition: To take goods knowing them to be stolen.
To act as a host for guests; to give admittance to; to permit to enter, as into one's house, presence, company, etc.
definitions.example: to receive a lodger, visitor, ambassador, messenger, etc.

definitions.definition: To incur (an injury).
definitions.example: I received a bloody nose from the collision.

definitions.definition: To allow (a custom, tradition, etc.); to give credence or acceptance to.
To detect a signal from a transmitter.
To be in a position to take possession, or hit back the ball.
To accept into the mind; to understand.
The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in some plants is able to perform vegetative reproduction.
definitions.example: This tree's roots can go as deep as twenty metres underground.

definitions.definition: A root vegetable.
The part of a tooth extending into the bone holding the tooth in place.
definitions.example: Root damage is a common problem of overbrushing.

definitions.definition: The part of a hair under the skin that holds the hair in place.
definitions.example: The root is the only part of the hair that is alive.

definitions.definition: The part of a hair near the skin that has not been dyed, permed, or otherwise treated.
definitions.example: He dyed his hair black last month, so the grey roots can be seen.

definitions.definition: The primary source; origin.
definitions.example: The love of money is the root of all evil.

definitions.definition: Of a number or expression, a number which, when raised to a specified power, yields the specified number or expression.
definitions.example: The cube root of 27 is 3.

definitions.definition: A square root (understood if no power is specified; in which case, “the root of” is often abbreviated to “root”).
definitions.example: Multiply by root 2.

definitions.definition: A zero (of an equation).
The single node of a tree that has no parent.
The primary lexical unit of a word, which carries the most significant aspects of semantic content and cannot be reduced into smaller constituents. Inflectional stems often derive from roots.
(philology) A word from which another word or words are derived.
The fundamental tone of any chord; the tone from whose harmonics, or overtones, a chord is composed.
The lowest place, position, or part.

In UNIX terminology, the first user account with complete access to the operating system and its configuration, found at the root of the directory structure; the person who manages accounts on a UNIX system.
definitions.example: I have to log in as root before I do that.

definitions.definition: The highest directory of a directory structure which may contain both files and subdirectories.
definitions.example: I installed the files in the root directory.

definitions.definition: A penis, especially the base of a penis.
To grow roots; to enter the earth, as roots; to take root and begin to grow.
definitions.example: The cuttings are starting to root.

definitions.definition: To prepare, oversee, or otherwise cause the rooting of cuttings
definitions.example: We rooted some cuttings last summer.

definitions.definition: To be firmly fixed; to be established.
To get root or priviledged access on a computer system or mobile phone, often through bypassing some security mechanism.
definitions.example: I want to root my Android phone so I can remove the preinstalled crapware.

definitions.definition: To add or substitute new parts or components to some device, structure etc., that were not previously available; to modernize
To fix an older version (or older versions) as part of the same process of fixing the newest version; to backport
definitions.example: The bug was so bad that we had to retrofit our patch to the last three releases, as well as the newest release.

definitions.definition: The process by which something is retrofitted.
State of nonexistence; the condition of being nothing.
Void; emptiness.
Quality of inconsequentiality; lacking in significance.
Any lizard of the family Gekkonidae. They are small, carnivorous, mostly nocturnal animals with large eyes and adhesive toes enabling them to climb on vertical and upside-down surfaces.
To move in the manner of a gecko; to attach to a vertical or upside-down surface.
A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.
A person who decides the fate of someone or something that has been called into question.
A person officiating at a sports event or similar.
definitions.example: At a boxing match, the decision of the judges is final.

definitions.definition: A person who evaluates something or forms an opinion.
definitions.example: She is a good judge of wine.

definitions.definition: Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.
A person of short stature, often one whose limbs are disproportionately small in relation to the body as compared with normal adults, usually as the result of a genetic condition.
An animal, plant or other thing much smaller than the usual of its sort.
definitions.example: dwarf honeysuckle

definitions.definition: A star of relatively small size.
To render (much) smaller, turn into a dwarf (version).
To make appear (much) smaller, puny, tiny.
definitions.example: The newly-built skyscraper dwarfs all older buildings in the downtown skyline.

definitions.definition: To make appear insignificant.
definitions.example: Bach dwarfs all other composers.

definitions.definition: To become (much) smaller.
To hinder from growing to the natural size; to make or keep small; to stunt.
Miniature.
definitions.example: It is possible to grow the plants as dwarf as one desires.

definitions.definition: A tree-dwelling marsupial, Phascolarctos cinereus, that resembles a small bear with a broad head, large ears and sharp claws, mainly found in eastern Australia.
An Okinawan martial art involving primarily punching and kicking, but additionally, advanced throws, arm bars, grappling and all means of fighting.
To attack (somebody or something) with karate or similar techniques.
To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.
definitions.example: Did you kick your brother?

definitions.definition: To make a sharp jerking movement of the leg, as to strike something.
definitions.example: He enjoyed the simple pleasure of watching the kickline kick.

definitions.definition: To direct to a particular place by a blow with the foot or leg.
definitions.example: Kick the ball into the goal.

definitions.definition: (with "off" or "out") To eject summarily.
To forcibly remove a participant from an online activity.
definitions.example: He was kicked by ChanServ for flooding.

definitions.definition: To overcome (a bothersome or difficult issue or obstacle); to free oneself of (a problem).
definitions.example: I still smoke, but they keep telling me to kick the habit.

definitions.definition: To move or push suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: He was kicked sideways by the force of the blast.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm) To recoil; to push by recoiling.
To attack (a piece) in order to force it to move.
To accelerate quickly with a few pedal strokes in an effort to break away from other riders.
definitions.example: Contador kicks again to try to rid himself of Rasmussen.

definitions.definition: To show opposition or resistance.
To work a press by impact of the foot on a treadle.
To die.
(smoking, of a pipe) Empty with nothing left to smoke but ash.
A container made of rigid material, often with a handle, used to carry liquids or small items.
definitions.example: I need a bucket to carry the water from the well.

definitions.definition: The amount held in this container.
definitions.example: The horse drank a whole bucket of water.

definitions.definition: A unit of measure equal to four gallons.
Part of a piece of machinery that resembles a bucket (container).
An old vehicle that is not in good working order.
The basket.
definitions.example: The forward drove to the bucket.

definitions.definition: A field goal.
definitions.example: We can't keep giving up easy buckets.

definitions.definition: (variation management) A mechanism for avoiding the allocation of targets in cases of mismanagement.
A storage space in a hash table for every item sharing a particular key.
(chiefly in the plural) A large amount of liquid.
definitions.example: I was so nervous that I sweated buckets.

definitions.definition: A bucket bag.
The leather socket for holding the whip when driving, or for the carbine or lance when mounted.
The pitcher in certain orchids.
To place inside a bucket.
To draw or lift in, or as if in, buckets.
definitions.example: to bucket water

definitions.definition: To rain heavily.
definitions.example: It’s really bucketing down out there.

definitions.definition: To travel very quickly.
definitions.example: The boat is bucketing along.

definitions.definition: To categorize (data) by splitting it into buckets, or groups of related items.
To ride (a horse) hard or mercilessly.
To make, or cause to make (the recovery), with a certain hurried or unskillful forward swing of the body.
To estimate the value of something; to appraise or to make a valuation.
Moving clockwise; having rotary motion in the manner of a clock.
definitions.example: In the southern hemisphere the flow of air around a low-pressure system is clockwise.

definitions.definition: In a curve or twist corresponding to the movement of the hands of a clock.
(usually in the plural) A pellet of dry cat food.
Likely to crunch, especially with reference to food when it is eaten.
Having sensibilities of a counter-culture nature lover or hippie; derived from the concept of crunchy granola.
definitions.example: San Francisco is a very crunchy town.

definitions.definition: A work, such as a blanket, composed of many different colors and shapes, sewn together to make an interesting whole.
Any kind of creation that utilizes many different aspects to create one whole piece.
A state of regulations whose constituents have an opaque scope of application because of their questionable delimitation with regard to each other.
To create a patchwork from pieces of fabric.
To assemble from a variety of sources; to cobble together.
A metal.
Any of several types of plant.
The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. (contrast morpheme.)
Something like such a unit of language:
The fact or act of speaking, as opposed to taking action. .
Something that someone said; a comment, utterance; speech.
A watchword or rallying cry, a verbal signal (even when consisting of multiple words).
definitions.example: mum's the word

definitions.definition: A proverb or motto.
News; tidings (used without an article).
definitions.example: Have you had any word from John yet?

definitions.definition: An order; a request or instruction; an expression of will.
definitions.example: Don't fire till I give the word

definitions.definition: A promise; an oath or guarantee.
definitions.example: I give you my word that I will be there on time.

definitions.definition: A brief discussion or conversation.
definitions.example: Can I have a word with you?

definitions.definition: (in the plural) See words.
definitions.example: There had been words between him and the secretary about the outcome of the meeting.

definitions.definition: (sometimes Word) Communication from God; the message of the Christian gospel; the Bible, Scripture.
definitions.example: Her parents had lived in Botswana, spreading the word among the tribespeople.

definitions.definition: (sometimes Word) Logos, Christ.
To say or write (something) using particular words; to phrase (something).
definitions.example: I’m not sure how to word this letter to the council.

definitions.definition: To flatter with words, to cajole.
To ply or overpower with words.
To conjure with a word.
To speak, to use words; to converse, to discourse.
Truth, indeed, that is the truth! The shortened form of the statement "My word is my bond."
definitions.example: "Yo, that movie was epic!" / "Word?" ("You speak the truth?") / "Word." ("I speak the truth.")

definitions.definition: (stereotypically) An abbreviated form of word up; a statement of the acknowledgment of fact with a hint of nonchalant approval.
The function or duty of an ambassador.
An organization or group of officials who permanently represent a sovereign state in a second sovereign state or with respect to an international organization such as the United Nations.
definitions.example: The American embassy to France is located in Paris.

definitions.definition: A temporary mission representing a sovereign state.
The official residence of such a group, or of an ambassador.
In spite of what preceded; yet.
Hopelessly outdated
definitions.example: What am I supposed to do with this neolithic piece of machinery?

definitions.definition: A poisoning, as by a spirituous or a narcotic substance.
definitions.example: He suffered acute intoxication from the combined effects of several drugs.

definitions.definition: The state of being intoxicated or drunk.
The act of intoxicating or making drunk.
A high excitement of mind; an elation which rises to enthusiasm, frenzy, or madness.
A white person from an English-speaking country, particularly the United States.
definitions.example: Gringoes, go home!

definitions.definition: A mark left by something that has passed along.
definitions.example: Can you see any tracks in the snow?

definitions.definition: A mark or impression left by the foot, either of man or animal.
definitions.example: The fox tracks were still visible in the snow.

definitions.definition: The entire lower surface of the foot; said of birds, etc.
A road or other similar beaten path.
definitions.example: Follow the track for a hundred metres.

definitions.definition: Physical course; way.
definitions.example: Astronomers predicted the track of the comet.

definitions.definition: A path or course laid out for a race, for exercise, etc.
definitions.example: The athletes ran round the track.

definitions.definition: The direction and progress of someone or something; path.
The way or rails along which a train moves.
definitions.example: They briefly closed the railway to remove debris found on the track.

definitions.definition: A tract or area, such as of land.
The street, as a prostitute's place of work.
Awareness of something, especially when arising from close monitoring.
The distance between two opposite wheels on a same axletree.
Short for caterpillar track.
The pitch.
Sound stored on a record.
The physical track on a record.
A song or other relatively short piece of music, on a record, separated from others by a short silence.
definitions.example: My favourite track on the album is "Sunshine".

definitions.definition: A circular (never-ending) data storage unit on a side of magnetic or optical disk, divided into sectors.
The racing events of track and field; track and field in general.
definitions.example: I'm going to try out for track next week.

definitions.definition: A session talk on a conference.
To continue over time.
To follow the tracks of.
definitions.example: My uncle spent all day tracking the deer, whose hoofprints were clear in the mud.

definitions.definition: To make tracks on.
To create a musical recording (a track).
definitions.example: Lil Kyle is gonna track with that DJ next week.

definitions.definition: To make sense; to be consistent with known information
A person whose job is making, repairing or selling jewelry.
An evil supernatural spirit.
A neutral supernatural spirit.
Someone with great strength, passion or skill for a particular activity, pursuit etc.; an enthusiast.
definitions.example: He’s a demon at the card tables.

definitions.definition: A form of patience (known as Canfield in the US).
Any of various hesperiid butterflies of the genera Notocrypta and Udaspes.
The front part of the head of a human or other animal, featuring the eyes, nose and mouth, and the surrounding area.
definitions.example: That girl has a pretty face.

definitions.definition: One's facial expression.
definitions.example: Why the sad face?

definitions.definition: (in expressions such as 'make a face') A distorted facial expression; an expression of displeasure, insult, etc.
definitions.example: Children! Stop making faces at each other!

definitions.definition: The public image; outward appearance.
definitions.example: He managed to show a bold face despite his embarrassment.

definitions.definition: The frontal aspect of something.
definitions.example: The face of the cliff loomed above them.

definitions.definition: An aspect of the character or nature of someone or something.
definitions.example: Poverty is the ugly face of capitalism.

definitions.definition: Presence; sight; front.
definitions.example: to fly in the face of danger

definitions.definition: The directed force of something.
definitions.example: They turned the boat into the face of the storm.

definitions.definition: Good reputation; standing in the eyes of others; dignity; prestige. (See lose face, save face).
Shameless confidence; boldness; effrontery.
definitions.example: You've got some face coming round here after what you've done.

definitions.definition: Any surface, especially a front or outer one.
definitions.example: Put a big sign on each face of the building that can be seen from the road.

definitions.definition: Any of the flat bounding surfaces of a polyhedron. More generally, any of the bounding pieces of a polytope of any dimension.
The numbered dial of a clock or watch, the clock face.
The mouth.
definitions.example: He's always stuffing his face with chips.

definitions.definition: Makeup; one's complete facial cosmetic application.
definitions.example: I'll be out in a sec. Just let me put on my face.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) A person.
definitions.example: It was just the usual faces at the pub tonight.

definitions.definition: A familiar or well-known person; a member of a particular scene, such as music or fashion scene.
definitions.example: He owned several local businesses and was a face around town.

definitions.definition: A headlining wrestler with a persona embodying heroic or virtuous traits and who is regarded as a "good guy", especially one who is handsome and well-conditioned; a baby face.
definitions.example: The fans cheered on the face as he made his comeback.

definitions.definition: The front surface of a bat.
The part of a golf club that hits the ball.
The side of the card that shows its value (as opposed to the back side, which looks the same on all cards of the deck).
The head of a lion, shown face-on and cut off immediately behind the ears.
The width of a pulley, or the length of a cog from end to end.
definitions.example: a pulley or cog wheel of ten inches face

definitions.definition: A typeface.
Mode of regard, whether favourable or unfavourable; favour or anger.
The amount expressed on a bill, note, bond, etc., without any interest or discount; face value.
(of a person or animal) To position oneself or itself so as to have one's face closest to (something).
definitions.example: Face the sun.

definitions.definition: (of an object) To have its front closest to, or in the direction of (something else).
definitions.example: Turn the chair so it faces the table.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to turn or present a face or front, as in a particular direction.
To be presented or confronted with; to have in prospect.
definitions.example: We are facing an uncertain future.

definitions.definition: To deal with (a difficult situation or person); to accept (facts, reality, etc.) even when undesirable.
definitions.example: I'm going to have to face this sooner or later.

definitions.definition: To have the front in a certain direction.
definitions.example: The seats in the carriage faced backwards.

definitions.definition: To have as an opponent.
definitions.example: Puddletown United face Mudchester Rovers in the quarter-finals.

definitions.definition: To be the batsman on strike.
definitions.example: Willoughby comes in to bowl, and it's Hobson facing.

definitions.definition: To confront impudently; to bully.
To cover in front, for ornament, protection, etc.; to put a facing upon.
definitions.example: a building faced with marble

definitions.definition: To line near the edge, especially with a different material.
definitions.example: to face the front of a coat, or the bottom of a dress

definitions.definition: To cover with better, or better appearing, material than the mass consists of, for purpose of deception, as the surface of a box of tea, a barrel of sugar, etc.

To make the surface of (anything) flat or smooth; to dress the face of (a stone, a casting, etc.); especially, in turning, to shape or smooth the flat surface of, as distinguished from the cylindrical surface.
(retail) To arrange the products in (a store) so that they are tidy and attractive.
definitions.example: In my first job, I learned how to operate a till and to face the store to high standards.

definitions.definition: A covering of liquid, particularly oil.
definitions.example: Careful in turn three — there's an oil slick on the road.

definitions.definition: Someone who is clever and untrustworthy.
A tool used to make something smooth or even.
A tire with a smooth surface instead of a tread pattern, often used in auto racing.
definitions.example: You'll go much faster if you put on slicks.

definitions.definition: A helicopter.
A camera-ready image to be used by a printer. The "slick" is photographed to produce a negative image which is then used to burn a positive offset plate or other printing device.
definitions.example: The project was delayed because the slick had not been delivered to the printer.

definitions.definition: A wide paring chisel used in joinery.
To make slick.
definitions.example: The surface had been slicked.

definitions.definition: Slippery or smooth due to a covering of liquid; often used to describe appearances.
definitions.example: His large round head was shaved slick.

definitions.definition: Appearing expensive or sophisticated.
definitions.example: They read all kinds of slick magazines.

definitions.definition: Superficially convincing but actually untrustworthy.
definitions.example: That new sales rep is slick. Be sure to read the fine print before you buy anything.

definitions.definition: (often used sarcastically) Clever, making an apparently hard task easy.
definitions.example: Our new process for extracting needles from haystacks is extremely slick.

definitions.definition: Extraordinarily great or special.
definitions.example: That is one slick bicycle: it has all sorts of features!

definitions.definition: Sleek; smooth
Impure carbon obtained by destructive distillation of wood or other organic matter, that is to say, heating it in the absence of oxygen.
A stick of black carbon material used for drawing.
A drawing made with charcoal.
A very dark gray colour.
To draw with charcoal.
To cook over charcoal.
Of a dark gray colour.
Made of charcoal.
Able to be played.
definitions.example: Not all compact discs are playable in car stereos.

definitions.definition: (games) Of a move, giving a reasonable result; able to be played without losing.
definitions.example: Is MAGICS a playable word in Scrabble?

definitions.definition: (games) Of a game, able to be played and enjoyed.
To pierce or puncture slightly.
definitions.example: John hardly felt the needle prick his arm when the adept nurse drew blood.

definitions.definition: To form by piercing or puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a pattern for embroidery

definitions.definition: To mark or denote by a puncture; to designate by pricking; to choose; to mark.
To mark the surface of (something) with pricks or dots; especially, to trace a ship’s course on (a chart).
To run a middle seam through the cloth of a sail.
To fix by the point; to attach or hang by puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a knife into a board

definitions.definition: To be punctured; to suffer or feel a sharp pain, as by puncture.
definitions.example: A sore finger pricks.

definitions.definition: To make or become sharp; to erect into a point; to raise, as something pointed; said especially of the ears of an animal, such as a horse or dog; and usually followed by up.
definitions.example: The dog's ears pricked up at the sound of a whistle.

definitions.definition: Usually in the form prick out: to plant (seeds or seedlings) in holes made in soil at regular intervals.
To incite, stimulate, goad.
To urge one's horse on; to ride quickly.
To affect with sharp pain; to sting, as with remorse.
To make acidic or pungent.
To become sharp or acid; to turn sour, as wine.
To aim at a point or mark.
Usually as prick up: to dress or adorn; to prink.
Punctured by small holes
A group or set of eight of something.
A group of eight musicians performing together.
definitions.example: An octet of waiters sang her "Happy Birthday".

definitions.definition: A composition for such a group of musicians.
A byte of eight bits. Abbreviation: o
A group of three bits, representing any of eight possible values.
On board; into or within a ship or boat; hence, into or within a railway car.
definitions.example: We all climbed aboard.

definitions.definition: On or onto a horse, a camel, etc.
definitions.example: To sling a saddle aboard.

definitions.definition: On base.
definitions.example: He doubled with two men aboard, scoring them both.

definitions.definition: Into a team, group, or company.
definitions.example: The office manager welcomed him aboard.

definitions.definition: Alongside.
definitions.example: The captain laid his ship aboard the enemy's ship.

definitions.definition: On board of; onto or into a ship, boat, train, plane.
definitions.example: We all went aboard the ship.

definitions.definition: Onto a horse.
Across; athwart; alongside.
An expression of remorse or regret for having said or done something that harmed another: an instance of apologizing (saying that one is sorry).
definitions.example: The CEO made a public apology for the scandal, and promised full cooperation with the authorities.

definitions.definition: A formal justification, defence.
definitions.example: The Apology of Socrates.

definitions.definition: Anything provided as a substitute; a makeshift.
definitions.example: a poor apology for a hotel room

definitions.definition: A guarantee that a certain outcome or obligation will be fulfilled; security.

An obsolete legal agreement that was a real covenant and ran with the land, whereby the grantor and his heirs of a piece of real estate held in freehold were required to officially guarantee their claim and plead one’s case for the title. If evicted by someone with a superior claim (paramount title) they were also required to hand over other real estate of equal value in recompense. It has now been replaced by personal covenants and the covenant of warranty.

A legal agreement, either written or oral (an expressed warranty) or implied through the actions of the buyer and seller (an implied warranty), which states that the goods or property in question will be in exactly the same state as promised, such as in a sale of an item or piece of real estate.
A written guarantee, usually over a fixed period, provided to someone who buys a product or item, which states that repairs will be provided free of charge in case of damage or a fault.
definitions.example: I made sure to check the terms of my warranty for my computer to ensure I was covered in case it broke down.

definitions.definition: (insurance law) A stipulation of an insurance policy made by an insuree, guaranteeing that the facts of the policy are true and the insurance risk is as stated, which if not fulfilled renders the policy void.
Justification or mandate to do something, especially in terms of one’s personal conduct.
To warrant; to guarantee.
An expression of mirth particular to the human species; the sound heard in laughing; laughter.
definitions.example: His deep laughs boomed through the room.

definitions.definition: Something that provokes mirth or scorn.
definitions.example: Your new hat's an absolute laugh, dude.

definitions.definition: A fun person.

To show mirth, satisfaction, or derision, by peculiar movement of the muscles of the face, particularly of the mouth, causing a lighting up of the face and eyes, and usually accompanied by the emission of explosive or chuckling sounds from the chest and throat; to indulge in laughter.
definitions.example: There were many laughing children running on the school grounds.

definitions.definition: To be or appear cheerful, pleasant, mirthful, lively, or brilliant; to sparkle; to sport.
(followed by "at") To make an object of laughter or ridicule; to make fun of; to deride; to mock.
definitions.example: Don't laugh at my new hat, man!

definitions.definition: To affect or influence by means of laughter or ridicule.
To express by, or utter with, laughter.
A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
definitions.example: Tigers and lions share a common ancestor from a few million years ago.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any of various extant and extinct big cats, especially the mountain lion.
A Chinese foo dog.
An individual who shows strength and courage, attributes associated with the lion.
A famous person regarded with interest and curiosity.
A light brown color that resembles the fur of a lion.
An old Scottish coin, with a lion on the obverse, worth 74 shillings.
An exhibition of works from an extended period of an artist's activity.
Of, relating to, or contemplating the past.
Looking backwards.
Affecting or influencing past things; retroactive.
To apply ink to; to cover or smear with ink.
To sign (a contract or similar document).
To apply a tattoo to (someone).
(of a squid or octopus) to eject ink (sense 3)
An application of ink.
Work done by an inker, one kind of commercial artist
Inputting text or drawings into a computer, tablet, or similar apparatus using a stylus.
A defensive activity where ink is released by squids, cuttlefish, etc.
A natural or inherent impulse or behaviour.
definitions.example: Many animals fear fire by instinct.

definitions.definition: An intuitive reaction not based on rational conscious thought.
definitions.example: Debbie's instinct was to distrust John.

definitions.definition: Imbued, charged (with something).
A piece of armour for the throat.
A type of women's clothing covering the neck and breast; a wimple.
An ornament for the neck; a necklace, ornamental collar, torque etc.
A cutting instrument used in lithotomy.
A grooved instrument used in performing various operations; called also blunt gorget.
A crescent-shaped coloured patch on the neck of a bird or mammal.
A hake caught in a net set for other fish.
Having a color.
definitions.example: Wash colored items separately from whites and darks to prevent the colors from bleeding.

definitions.definition: Having a particular color or kind of color.
definitions.example: The room was red, with a dark-colored rug.

definitions.definition: Having prominent colors; colorful.
definitions.example: The singer wore a colored shirt.

definitions.definition: Influenced pervasively but subtly.
definitions.example: My opinions are colored by my upbringing.

definitions.definition: Of skin color other than white; in particular, black.
Of neither black nor white skin color.
definitions.example: Most of the colored community speaks Afrikaans, whereas languages like Xhosa or Venda are typically spoken by blacks and English is spoken mostly by whites.

definitions.definition: Designated for use by colored people (in either the US or South African sense).
definitions.example: a colored drinking fountain

definitions.definition: An achromatic colour intermediate between black and white.
An animal or thing of grey colour, such as a horse, badger, or salmon.
An extraterrestrial humanoid with greyish skin, bulbous black eyes, and an enlarged head.
To become grey.
definitions.example: My hair is beginning to grey.

definitions.definition: To cause to become grey.
To turn progressively older, in the context of the population of a geographic region.
definitions.example: the greying of Europe

definitions.definition: To give a soft effect to (a photograph) by covering the negative while printing with a ground-glass plate.
Having a color somewhere between white and black, as the ash of an ember.
Dreary, gloomy.
Having an indistinct, disputed or uncertain quality.
Relating to older people.
definitions.example: the grey dollar, i.e. the purchasing power of the elderly

definitions.definition: A ceremony to honour and remember a deceased person. Often distinguished from a memorial service by the presence of the body of the deceased.
definitions.example: Many mourners turned up at the local artist's funeral to pay homage.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) A funeral sermon.
Of or relating to a funeral.
Similar to a funeral in mood; dignified or solemn.
A colorful, conspicuous structure associated with angiosperms, frequently scented and attracting various insects, and which may or may not be used for sexual reproduction.
A reproductive structure in angiosperms (flowering plants), often conspicuously colourful and typically including sepals, petals, and either or both stamens and/or a pistil.
A plant that bears flowers, especially a plant that is small and lacks wood.
definitions.example: We transplanted the flowers to a larger pot.

definitions.definition: (usually with in) Of plants, a state of bearing blooms.
definitions.example: The dogwoods are in flower this week.

definitions.definition: (hypocoristic) The vulva, especially the labia majora.
The best examples or representatives of a group.
definitions.example: We selected the flower of the applicants.

definitions.definition: The best state of things; the prime.
definitions.example: She was in the flower of her life.

definitions.definition: Flour.
(in the plural) A substance in the form of a powder, especially when condensed from sublimation.
definitions.example: the flowers of sulphur

definitions.definition: A figure of speech; an ornament of style.
Ornamental type used chiefly for borders around pages, cards, etc.
(in the plural) Menstrual discharges.
To put forth blooms.
definitions.example: This plant flowers in June.

definitions.definition: To decorate with pictures of flowers.
To reach a state of full development or achievement.
To froth; to ferment gently, as new beer.
To come off as flowers by sublimation.
Something that flows, such as a river.
